jokes.text: This will be, like, the ninth time in a row.
jokes.setup: Larry apologizing to comedian Jeff Cesario for running out of time
jokes.punchline: The casual admission that this keeps happening repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Larry's casual cruelty delivered matter-of-factly. Sets up the episode's central conflict.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: This will be, like, the ninth time in a row.

jokes.text: He was loaded.
jokes.setup: Making excuses for why the comedian didn't see the wrap signal
jokes.punchline: Blunt confirmation of the worst possible explanation
jokes.explanation: Economical and deadpan, typical Larry Sanders dry delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: He was loaded.

jokes.text: You've bumped me clean out of the young comedian category.
jokes.setup: Larry calling 42-year-old Jeff a 'bright, young comedian'
jokes.punchline: Jeff's bitter realization about aging out of categories
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'bumped' - both literally bumped from the show and metaphorically bumped from youth.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Cesario
dialogue.line: You've bumped me clean out of the young comedian category.

jokes.text: Christ, I feel like George Miller.
jokes.setup: Jeff's frustration at being repeatedly bumped
jokes.punchline: Reference to another comedian presumably known for getting bumped
jokes.explanation: Industry insider reference that rings true for the show business setting.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Cesario
dialogue.line: Christ, I feel like George Miller.

jokes.text: Supremely bumpable.
jokes.setup: Discussing which guests can be bumped from the show
jokes.punchline: Artie's clinical assessment of David Duchovny's bumpability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie line - ruthlessly practical with invented TV industry terminology.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Supremely bumpable.

jokes.text: Well, I'm going let that bit of nonsense pass.
jokes.setup: Beverly mentioning People Magazine calling Duchovny the next James Dean
jokes.punchline: Larry's dismissive, superior attitude
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry pomposity - acting magnanimous while being petty.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, I'm going let that bit of nonsense pass.

jokes.text: Well, if I cut my monologue down, it takes me three minutes to get going to begin with, so if I cut it down by two minutes, I'm only going to have one minute where it really works.
jokes.setup: Beverly suggesting Larry trim his monologue to make room
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-defeating logic about his own performance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Larry's neurotic self-analysis reveals his insecurity while being completely self-absorbed.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, if I cut my monologue down, it takes me three minutes to get going to begin with, so if I cut it down by two minutes, I'm only going to have one minute where it really works.

jokes.text: Rob Lowe.
jokes.setup: Hank asking who's more important than his promise to his dead father
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate, matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff - the brutal honesty of show business priorities delivered with zero hesitation.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Rob Lowe.

jokes.text: All right. More pussy for Larry.
jokes.setup: Larry mentioning Vendela as unbumpable
jokes.punchline: Hank's inappropriate grief-fueled outburst
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable moment that reveals Hank's inappropriate coping mechanism.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: All right. More pussy for Larry.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. That's not me. You know that's not me. That's just the grief talking.
jokes.setup: Hank's crude comment about Vendela
jokes.punchline: His immediate backpedal blaming grief
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Hank's weak attempt to excuse his behavior while still manipulating the situation.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I'm sorry. That's not me. You know that's not me. That's just the grief talking.

jokes.text: The chef? Come on.
jokes.setup: Someone mentioning David Duchovny
jokes.punchline: Hank's confusion about who Duchovny is
jokes.explanation: Simple confusion joke that shows Hank's disconnect from pop culture.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: The chef? Come on.

jokes.text: Again? What is he, homeless? Get a job.
jokes.setup: Learning Jeff Cesario is also on the show
jokes.punchline: Hank's callous dismissal of a struggling comedian
jokes.explanation: Brutal honesty about the comedy business hierarchy delivered with typical Hank insensitivity.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Again? What is he, homeless? Get a job.

jokes.text: So if I talk about it another day, it's not gonna be topical.
jokes.setup: Larry suggesting Hank do his father tribute another day
jokes.punchline: Hank's twisted logic about topicality of death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - treating personal grief like show business content with perfect Hank reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: So if I talk about it another day, it's not gonna be topical.

jokes.text: Let's, uh, give him 2½ minutes, and, uh, we'll make it work.
jokes.setup: Hank negotiating down from 4 minutes to 3 minutes for his tribute
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately cutting it further without asking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry move - agreeing then immediately undermining, showing his complete lack of respect for boundaries.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Let's, uh, give him 2½ minutes, and, uh, we'll make it work.

jokes.text: His old man abandoned him when he was 2.
jokes.setup: Someone expressing surprise that Hank hated his father
jokes.punchline: The reveal of Hank's actual relationship with his father
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation that reframes Hank's tribute as potentially fraudulent manipulation.

dialogue.line: His old man abandoned him when he was 2.

jokes.text: I want you to check and make sure that Hank's father really died.
jokes.setup: Learning about Hank's complicated relationship with his father
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate suspicion and cynicism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry paranoia - immediately assuming the worst of everyone around him.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I want you to check and make sure that Hank's father really died.

jokes.text: Let's not get too cynical. Please?
jokes.setup: Larry's paranoid request to verify Hank's father's death
jokes.punchline: Beverly's plea against cynicism in this completely cynical environment
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic moment - asking for less cynicism while surrounded by completely justified cynicism.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Let's not get too cynical. Please?

jokes.text: blah, blah, blah, blah.
jokes.setup: Phil reading his attempt at writing Hank's father tribute
jokes.punchline: Phil literally saying 'blah blah blah' in the middle of the tribute
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly awkward - showing the complete hollowness of manufactured sentiment by literally filling it with nonsense.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: blah, blah, blah, blah.

jokes.text: He was a great man. That's all you need to know.
jokes.setup: Phil asking for details about Hank's father to write the tribute
jokes.punchline: Hank's defensive non-answer
jokes.explanation: Reveals Hank's inability to provide any real details, suggesting the tribute is hollow or fraudulent.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: He was a great man. That's all you need to know.

jokes.text: Yeah, right. Did he show up at your high school graduation?
jokes.setup: Phil asking about Hank's relationship with his father for the tribute
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive question revealing Hank's father abandoned him
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - Phil knows the real story and is being sardonic.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Yeah, right. Did he show up at your high school graduation?

jokes.text: Well, I just wanna know when you're gonna have some black people on.
jokes.setup: Larry asking Beverly what she thinks they should do about the booking situation
jokes.punchline: Beverly's completely unrelated concern about diversity
jokes.explanation: Perfect non-sequitur that reveals Beverly's different priorities while everyone else is focused on logistics.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Well, I just wanna know when you're gonna have some black people on.

jokes.text: Oh, I just love it when you take control.
jokes.setup: Larry making a decisive choice to bump Cesario
jokes.punchline: Beverly's sarcastic appreciation
jokes.explanation: Mild sarcasm about Larry's leadership style, typical Beverly observation.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Oh, I just love it when you take control.

jokes.text: You're prettier.
jokes.setup: Larry on the phone admitting he thinks Vendela is pretty
jokes.punchline: His obvious lie to placate his wife
jokes.explanation: Standard married man deflection, made funny by the context and Larry's transparent insincerity.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You're prettier.

jokes.text: If those are his parents, he probably brought them in to put more fucking pressure on you.
jokes.setup: Learning that Jeff's parents are there to watch the show
jokes.punchline: Artie's cynical interpretation of the family visit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie cynicism - seeing manipulation in a normal family gesture, probably correctly.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: If those are his parents, he probably brought them in to put more fucking pressure on you.

jokes.text: I didn't come here just to stick my thumb up my ass.
jokes.setup: Duchovny explaining his frustration with potentially being bumped
jokes.punchline: His crude, frustrated outburst
jokes.explanation: Effective frustration line that shows how the bumping process affects guests.

dialogue.speaker: David Duchovny
dialogue.line: I didn't come here just to stick my thumb up my ass.

jokes.text: He'll bellow like a bull moose.
jokes.setup: Deciding who should talk to Hank about his tribute
jokes.punchline: Artie's colorful description of Hank's likely reaction
jokes.explanation: Vivid animal metaphor that perfectly captures Hank's dramatic tendencies.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: He'll bellow like a bull moose.

jokes.text: you're gonna bump my dead father?
jokes.setup: Larry starting to suggest schedule changes
jokes.punchline: Hank's dramatic interpretation as bumping the dead father
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - treating the tribute like a guest that can be bumped. Perfect Hank manipulation through dramatic language.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: you're gonna bump my dead father?

jokes.text: Well, princess won't work, 'cause that's what he calls me.
jokes.setup: Vendela listing nicknames her friends call her
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation about Larry's pet name
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail that reveals Larry's relationship with his guests and adds awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Vendela
dialogue.line: Well, princess won't work, 'cause that's what he calls me.

jokes.text: Well, my friends started callin' me doll baby, peanut, princess.
jokes.setup: Larry asks what Vendela's friends call her
jokes.punchline: List of cutesy nicknames
jokes.explanation: Setup for the next joke - not funny on its own but necessary.

dialogue.speaker: Vendela
dialogue.line: Well, my friends started callin' me doll baby, peanut, princess.

jokes.text: She's really flirting with me. Yeah. No shit.
jokes.setup: Hank observing Vendela's behavior
jokes.punchline: Artie's sarcastic confirmation
jokes.explanation: Simple setup/payoff showing Hank's obliviousness and Artie's dry humor.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: She's really flirting with me.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Yeah. No shit.

jokes.text: I suggest the infomercial bit. It's been done to death.
jokes.setup: Jeff being asked to cut 2 minutes from his set
jokes.punchline: Artie's blunt creative criticism
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie - brutally honest about comedy material quality.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I suggest the infomercial bit. It's been done to death.

jokes.text: But this has Jack to do with him. It's got to do with you grabbing a little glory in the spotlight.
jokes.setup: Hank insisting on tribute time for his father
jokes.punchline: Artie calling out Hank's real motivation
jokes.explanation: Artie cutting through the BS - he sees Hank's grief as opportunism.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: But this has Jack to do with him. It's got to do with you grabbing a little glory in the spotlight.

jokes.text: It's got to do with you grabbing a little glory in the spotlight.
jokes.setup: Hank claiming his tribute has nothing to do with his father
jokes.punchline: Artie cutting through the manipulation to the truth
jokes.explanation: Artie at his best - seeing through Hank's emotional manipulation to the selfish core.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: It's got to do with you grabbing a little glory in the spotlight.

jokes.text: Next time I'll use a trash bin.
jokes.setup: Statistics about syringes and condoms found during beach cleanup
jokes.punchline: Larry taking personal responsibility as a joke
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline structure with good economy. Standard late-night monologue joke.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Next time I'll use a trash bin.

jokes.text: And if that doesn't prove the theory of evolution, I don't know what does.
jokes.setup: Story about gorilla being chased and slapped by females
jokes.punchline: Larry's observation about evolution
jokes.explanation: Good callback to human relationship dynamics through animal behavior observation.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: And if that doesn't prove the theory of evolution, I don't know what does.

jokes.text: but that the light show was 'a killer, dude.'
jokes.setup: Person struck by lightning at Grateful Dead concert
jokes.punchline: The victim's assessment using 'killer' ironically
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'killer' - literally deadly lightning described as great entertainment.

dialogue.line: but that the light show was 'a killer, dude.'

jokes.text: And upon regaining consciousness, one of the victims said he thought the concert was kind of short, but that the light show was 'a killer, dude.'
jokes.setup: Grateful Dead fans struck by lightning
jokes.punchline: Victim's oblivious reaction calling lightning 'killer'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on 'killer' - works on multiple levels with the hippie characterization.

dialogue.line: And upon regaining consciousness, one of the victims said he thought the concert was kind of short, but that the light show was 'a killer, dude.'

jokes.text: Larry, my wife lets me talk more than that.
jokes.setup: Larry cutting Rob Lowe's segment very short
jokes.punchline: Rob's complaint comparing the show to his marriage
jokes.explanation: Great guest comeback - comparing talk show time to marriage dynamics with perfect timing.

dialogue.speaker: Rob Lowe
dialogue.line: Larry, my wife lets me talk more than that.

jokes.text: This is a talk show. OK?
jokes.setup: Rob Lowe's complaint about not getting to talk
jokes.punchline: Larry's defensive explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - defending a talk show where guests don't get to talk.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: This is a talk show. OK?

jokes.text: Well, you know, uh, marriage is about, uh, communication. This is a talk show. OK?
jokes.setup: Rob Lowe complains about being cut short
jokes.punchline: Larry's defensive distinction between marriage and his show
jokes.explanation: Larry's defensive logic - distinguishing his professional rudeness from personal relationships.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, you know, uh, marriage is about, uh, communication. This is a talk show. OK?

jokes.text: I don't want you to open it right now. You don't want me to open it now? No. I like the element of surprise.
jokes.setup: Vendela giving Larry a present on air
jokes.punchline: Awkward flirtation playing out on live television
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor from inappropriate on-air flirtation.

dialogue.speaker: Vendela
dialogue.line: I don't want you to open it right now.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You don't want me to open it now?

dialogue.speaker: Vendela
dialogue.line: No. I like the element of surprise.

jokes.text: I will not be returning this.
jokes.setup: Vendela giving Larry a gift with her phone number
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate, inappropriate response
jokes.explanation: Larry's inappropriate eagerness made funnier by the on-air context.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I will not be returning this.

jokes.text: That was so rude.
jokes.setup: Larry cutting her off mid-story
jokes.punchline: Her direct criticism of Larry's hosting
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective guest pushback against Larry's poor hosting behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Vendela
dialogue.line: That was so rude.

jokes.text: So that's what we had in the beginning. We had a cult following. Yeah. So really bad ratings is a cult following.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about X-Files having a cult following
jokes.punchline: Duchovny's cynical definition of cult following
jokes.explanation: Brilliant industry insight delivered as comedy - redefining TV marketing speak in realistic terms.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: So that's what we had in the beginning. We had a cult following. Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: David Duchovny
dialogue.line: So really bad ratings is a cult following.

jokes.text: So I'd rather just show the clip and then maybe explain it after. No, no. That was a good explanation anyway.
jokes.setup: Duchovny wanting to show a clip instead of explaining more
jokes.punchline: Larry dismissing the clip entirely
jokes.explanation: Larry's terrible hosting - dismissing what the guest wants to do.

dialogue.speaker: David Duchovny
dialogue.line: So I'd rather just show the clip and then maybe explain it after.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No, no. That was a good explanation anyway.

jokes.text: We can misplace anything, so help me.
jokes.setup: Claiming they lost Duchovny's clip

jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor that's actually an excuse for poor preparation.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: We can misplace anything, so help me.

jokes.text: You didn't lose that fucking clip.
jokes.setup: The claim about losing his clip
jokes.punchline: Duchovny calling out the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Guest seeing through the show's manipulation and calling it out directly.

dialogue.speaker: David Duchovny
dialogue.line: You didn't lose that fucking clip.

jokes.text: Put him on the Sean Young list.
jokes.setup: Duchovny asking to be on the show again

jokes.explanation: Perfect industry insider reference - Sean Young was known for being difficult to work with.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Put him on the Sean Young list.

jokes.text: I made it!
jokes.setup: Jeff finally getting to perform after being bumped repeatedly
jokes.punchline: His triumphant announcement
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the opening - celebrating finally making it on air after being bumped nine times.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Cesario
dialogue.line: I made it!

jokes.explanation: The infomercial bit Artie said was 'done to death' - proves his point.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Cesario
dialogue.line: Maybe I wanna cut my own hair with my own vacuum cleaner.

jokes.explanation: Dark moment revealing Hank's true feelings about his father - finding his death cause funny rather than sad.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Liver failure. Ha ha ha ha.

jokes.setup: Jeff's stand-up routine running over time

jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank selfishness - making everything about himself, even another performer's success.

dialogue.speaker: Hank

dialogue.line: you know, my dad was kind of an ass, too, but, hey, guys... we just got him a crappy Christmas gift, OK?

jokes.explanation: Dark but effective - combines true crime with hunting incompetence.

dialogue.line: It took 'em 40 shots to kill their parents and they were sittin' on the couch. Come on.

jokes.text: I really actually didn't know this man. So I don't, um... I don't wanna do this.
jokes.setup: Hank trying to deliver his father's tribute on air
jokes.punchline: His complete breakdown and admission
jokes.explanation: Powerful moment where Hank's manipulation collapses into honest vulnerability. Perfect character resolution.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I really actually didn't know this man. So I don't, um... I don't wanna do this.

jokes.text: I really actually didn't know this man. So I don't, um... I don't wanna do this. So back to you. I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Hank finally gets to do his tribute
jokes.punchline: He breaks down and admits he didn't know his father
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff - all of Hank's manipulation collapses into genuine emotion. Peak cringe and character work.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I really actually didn't know this man. So I don't, um... I don't wanna do this. So back to you. I'm sorry.

jokes.text: Why don't we, uh... take a moment for, uh, head... uh, for Hank's, uh, dead dad?
jokes.setup: Having too much time left after Hank's failed tribute
jokes.punchline: Larry's fumbled attempt to fill time

dialogue.speaker: Larry

jokes.text: Jesus, Larry, was it good? You're kidding, now, aren't you?
jokes.setup: Larry asking if his handling of the show ending was good
jokes.punchline: Artie's incredulous response

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Jesus, Larry, was it good? You're kidding, now, aren't you?
